intro.title: Introduction
intro.subtitle: Accounting and Financial Solutions That Drive Results
intro.text: In an increasingly competitive market, having personalized accounting and financial advisory services is essential for sustainable business growth. Our approach goes beyond regulatory compliance - we deliver strategic insights, organization, and reliable support to help each client make informed decisions, minimize risks, and maximize opportunities. We work closely with your business, tailoring solutions to meet your specific needs.

vision.title: Vision
vision.text: To be recognized nationally and internationally as a reference in strategic, transparent and humane accounting, especially supporting international companies in their journey of sustainable growth and compliance in Brazil. Open Book aims to build a legacy of longevity, being remembered for having a deep knowledge of its clients' business, anticipating their needs and acting as a reliable partner in business development, succession planning and business continuity, with ethics, transparency and commitment to the long term.
points: Reference in clear and human-centered accounting
Support for international companies in Brazil
Partnership that understands and anticipates needs
Focus on leadership, ethics, and business continuity

mission.title: Mission
mission.text: Our mission is to support our clients in the sustainable planning of their businesses, through the provision of regular accounting and tax services, with the delivery of analytical accounting and financial reports and statements, reliable, comparable and in line with legality, transparency and compliance standards, effectively contributing to strategic decision-making and the growth of their managers and organizations.
points: Ensure transparent accounting and tax services

values.title: Values
values.text: Open Book operates based on transparency, ethics and commitment to the client, seeking to deeply understand their business. It values innovation responsibly, combining technology with the human dimension, and continuously invests in the development of its team. Its decisions are guided by data, always focusing on sustainable and long-term results.

workStyle.title: Differentiated Way of Working
workStyle.subtitle: Hybrid Work: Balancing Flexibility, Productivity, and Engagement
workStyle.text: Since the pandemic, Open Book has adopted a hybrid work model, combining technology with personalized, human-centered service. This structure brings benefits for everyone.

benefits.employees: Employees gain better quality of life, flexibility, and increased productivity.
benefits.clients: Clients enjoy faster service, easier access, and tailor-made solutions.

workStyle.conclusion: With a solid technological foundation, we maintain transparent, secure, and efficient services. We continue to grow with a focus on trust, innovation, and close relationships with our clients.

partners.title: Message from the Partners
partners.text: With more than 13 years of experience, Open-Book Accounting and Financial Advisory offers customized accounting and financial solutions for companies of different sizes, operating in Brazil and abroad. The company has a team specialized in national and international accounting standards, taxes and financial management, providing complete and strategic service.
partners.text2: In addition to traditional accounting services, Open-Book also outsources financial management, with a focus on planning, control, and performance. Its performance is based on transparency, reliability and results orientation, supporting decisions with accurate and up-to-date data, always aligned with the principles of open accounting.

taxReform.author: Open-Book Accounting and Financial Advisory
taxReform.title: Tax reform and real estate
taxReform.subtitle: What changes in practice for property owners, investors and companies in the real estate sector.
taxReform.intro: The tax reform has brought significant changes to the real estate sector and requires attention from property owners, investors, asset holding companies, developers, subdividers, landlords and companies working with real estate. With the reform, various real estate operations are now expressly covered by the new IBS and CBS system, which tends to alter costs, margins, pricing and asset structures.
taxReform.operationsText: Among the operations with the greatest potential impact are:
operations: Non-onerous property transactions with partners and spouses
taxReform.operationsNote: Additionally, other relevant taxes continue to exist, such as ITBI, ITCMD, IPTU, ITR and Income Tax. This means that the tax analysis of real estate will become even more strategic, as the same operation may involve taxation on consumption, property and income simultaneously.
taxReform.rentalTitle: Property rental: one of the most sensitive points
taxReform.rentalText: Property rental deserves special attention. Under the new system, it is now part of the specific regime for real estate, subject to IBS and CBS, albeit with a legal 70% rate reduction. In practice, this may directly affect:
rentalItems: Real estate funds, depending on applicable classification
taxReform.rentalNote: An optional simplified taxation regime has also been created for certain leases, with a 3.65% rate on gross revenue, subject to specific legal and contractual conditions.
taxReform.reducersText: The new legislation provides mechanisms to reduce the tax impact on real estate:
reducers: Constitutional 50% reduction for certain real estate operations
Legal 70% reduction for leasing, assignment and renting
Social reducer for residential property situations
Adjustment reducer for the economic transition of the system
taxReform.reducersNote: These mechanisms can represent significant tax relief, but their application depends on the type of operation, the taxpayer's profile and the correct legal classification.
taxReform.recommendationsTitle: What we recommend at this time
taxReform.recommendationsText: Given this new scenario, it is advisable to review in advance:
recommendations: Development, subdivision and real estate projects
Asset and succession planning involving properties
Pricing models and tax burden pass-through
taxReform.assessmentText1: The tax reform does not eliminate the importance of real estate planning — on the contrary, it makes this planning even more necessary.
taxReform.assessmentText2: More than identifying whether there is a tax incidence, it will be essential to understand which tax applies, at what moment, on what basis, with what reductions and with what effective economic impact.

sinief.author: Tax Team — Open-Book
sinief.title: SINIEF Adjustment No. 49/2025
sinief.subtitle: Is your company ready for the new NF-e issuance rules?
sinief.intro1: Starting May 4, 2026, SINIEF Adjustment No. 49/2025 comes into effect, establishing new guidelines for issuing Electronic Tax Invoices (NF-e) in operational situations that, in practice, often generate doubts, inconsistencies and significant tax risks.
sinief.intro2: The regulation seeks to standardize nationally the tax treatment of some occurrences that are quite common in companies' routines. Although the topic may seem purely operational at first glance, its effects go far beyond invoice issuance — directly impacting tax, accounting, commercial, logistics, inventory, transportation and technology departments.
sinief.changesText: SINIEF Adjustment No. 49/2025 reinforces the need for each tax document to correctly represent the event that actually occurred. Key points requiring attention:

changes.title: Sale for future delivery with advance payment:
changes.text: the operation may require separate documents for the financial stage and for the actual shipment of goods.

changes.title: Inventory write-off due to loss, spoilage, deterioration, theft or robbery:
changes.text: now requires formal documentary treatment and adequate supporting evidence.

changes.title: Subsequent reduction of values or quantities:
changes.text: when it is no longer possible to cancel the original NF-e.

changes.title: Return of goods due to refusal or recipient not found:
changes.text: requiring compliance with electronic events related to failed delivery.

sinief.attentionTitle: Why does this require immediate attention?
sinief.attentionText1: Practical experience shows that these situations are often handled inconsistently by companies, frequently with informal solutions, manual adjustments or outdated system configurations.
sinief.attentionWarning: Inadequate issuance of tax documents can generate discrepancies between NF-e, physical inventory and bookkeeping, difficulty in proving losses, documentary weakness in returns, risks of disallowance or challenges regarding ICMS.
sinief.attentionText2: Not every situation can be resolved by canceling a tax invoice or issuing a correction letter. In many cases, regularization will require a specific tax document, referencing the previous operation.
sinief.impactTitle: Will all companies be equally impacted?
sinief.impactText: Not necessarily. The impact depends on each company's operational model, the type of merchandise, the frequency of returns, the existence of scheduled sales, the inventory control method, the logistics structure and even how the ERP is currently configured. Practical application requires individualized analysis.
sinief.prepareText: Now is the right time to review internal procedures and verify whether systems are prepared to properly support the new requirements. Special attention for companies with:
prepareItems: Frequent return and commercial adjustment routines
sinief.ctaTitle: Our team can support your company
sinief.ctaText: Our team is monitoring the impacts of SINIEF Adjustment No. 49/2025 and can assist your company in the practical analysis of the regulation, review of operational and tax flows, identification of risk points and adequacy of internal and documentary procedures.
sinief.ctaSignature: Open Book Accounting and Financial Advisory — Tax Team

objectivesList: Present clearly and strategically the main impacts of the Tax Reform on industry
Understand how the Dual VAT, CBS/IBS, split payment and new credit rules affect prices, margins, cash flow and decision-making
Prepare senior management to lead the tax transition and adapt internal processes

Review of contracts and purchases

sections.title: Pricing and Margins

cbenef.author: Tax Team — Open-Book
cbenef.title: Mandatory cBenef Field in NF-e and NFC-e in Sao Paulo
cbenef.subtitle: Ordinance SRE 70/2025: new requirement for electronic tax documents starting 04/06/2026.
cbenef.intro: We inform you of an important update to the tax legislation of the State of Sao Paulo regarding the issuance of electronic tax documents.
cbenef.contextText: According to Ordinance SRE 70/2025, published on 10/21/2025, starting 04/06/2026, it becomes mandatory to fill in the cBenef field (Tax Benefit Code) in NF-e (model 55) and NFC-e (model 65) whenever the operation is covered by a tax benefit. The regulation covers operations involving exemption, non-incidence, tax base reduction, special taxation regime on gross revenue, suspension and deferral.
scopeList: Special taxation regime on gross revenue
cbenef.impactText: The absence of the cBenef field may cause rejection of electronic invoice authorization, impacting the issuance of NF-e (DANFE model 55) and NFC-e (model 65). Valid codes must follow the CST x cBenef table provided by the Sao Paulo tax authority.
cbenef.attentionText: This adjustment will require a review of tax master data, CST parameterizations, tax rules and ERP issuance routines to correctly identify which operations have tax benefits and which code should be included in the XML in each case.
recommendationsList: Review all tax-benefited operations performed by the company
Identify the legal classification and the applicable cBenef code
Validate with the system/ERP provider the field parameterization in the XML
Test NF-e and NFC-e issuance in advance before the mandatory date
Update the tax and billing team on the new rules
cbenef.ctaText: We remain available to assist in analyzing the company's operations, mapping applicable codes and validating the parameterizations required to comply with the new legal requirement.

modules.title: Practical Cases and Simulations (1h)

methodologyList: Discussion applied to the legal context

nr1Responsabilidade.title: NR-1 in Practice: Civil and Criminal Liability in the Workplace
nr1Responsabilidade.subtitle: Understand when risk management failures lead to liability — and how to protect yourself.
nr1Responsabilidade.intro: This intensive course was designed to condense the essential content of NR-1 and legal liability in OHS, with a practical, decision-making and strategic focus. The goal is not just to teach the regulation, but to answer the central question: when does a risk management failure lead to liability — and how can you protect yourself?

modules.title: NR-1 as the Basis for Liability (2h)
items: NR-1 structure and its systemic role
Employer rights and obligations
Principles of prevention and risk management
Interface with CLT and other NRs
When NR-1 goes from technical regulation to source of legal liability

modules.title: GRO and PGR as Evidence and Defense (2h)
items: GRO and PGR structure: risk inventory and action plan
Integration with PCMSO and training programs
PGR as a management tool and evidence in legal proceedings
Critical errors: formal PGR without effectiveness, lack of documentary evidence

modules.title: Workplace Accidents and Employer Liability (2h)
items: Concept of accident and occupational disease
Causal nexus and concurrent causation
Subjective and objective liability
Culpa in vigilando, in eligendo and in omittendo
Indemnities, accident-related job stability and INSS regressive action

modules.title: Liability of OHS Professionals (1h30)
items: Engineer vs. Technician: scope of practice
Liability for reports and documents
Omission and technical failure
When the professional is held individually, jointly liable or excluded

modules.title: Administrative, Civil and Criminal Liability (1h30)
items: Infraction notices and inspections
Technical evidence and expert testimony
Criminal liability: negligent bodily harm, negligent homicide, endangerment crimes
Guarantor position and relevant criminal omission
Integrated practical case: accident analysis with PGR failure

resultsList: Identify legal risks before they materialize
Assess whether the company is legally exposed
Use PGR as a protection tool
Understand who is liable — and why
Make preventive decisions with confidence
formatList: Duration: 8 hours (1 day or 2 sessions of 4 hours)
Mode: in-person, live online or hybrid
nr1Responsabilidade.differentialText: Integrates Labor Law, Civil Liability, Criminal Liability, Safety Engineering and Risk Management. Based on NR-1, CLT, Civil Code, Criminal Code, Law 8.213/91 and current case law. Focus on real liability, not just theory.
nr1Responsabilidade.ctaText: Applicable course for liability prevention, process defense and OHS audits. Contact us for information.

nr1Psicossocial.title: NR-1 and Psychosocial Risk in Organizations
nr1Psicossocial.subtitle: Prevention, Management and Legal Liability in Companies.
nr1Psicossocial.intro: NR-1 now requires a new approach from organizations. Today, it is not enough to comply with formal workplace safety regulations — it is necessary to identify, manage and document psychosocial risks. Pressure for targets, burnout, harassment, overwork and toxic environments are already generating labor convictions, manager liability and increased social security costs.

modules.title: NR-1, GRO and PGR — Practical and Strategic Overview (2h)
items: NR-1 structure in the OHS system
GRO (Occupational Risk Management)
PGR: essential structure and practical application
Risk inventory and action plan
Integration with governance, compliance and legal evidence

modules.title: Psychosocial Risk: Identification and Impact (2h)
items: Concept of psychosocial risk and difference between risk and illness
Critical organizational factors: abusive targets, overwork, performance pressure
Moral harassment and psychological violence
Burnout, stress and mental fatigue
Indicators of organizational illness

modules.title: Mental Health and Employer Liability (2h)
items: Healthy work environment (Constitution + CLT)
Duty of prevention (general duty of care)
Organizational culture and management failures
Liability: labor, civil, social security and criminal
Causal nexus and concurrent causation

modules.title: Practical Implementation and Liability Reduction (2h)
items: How to map psychosocial risk
How to document properly (evidence)
PGR in practice including psychosocial risk
Training, policies and reporting channels
Integration between HR, legal and OHS departments
Applied case study

resultsList: Identify psychosocial risks in your organization
Understand where legal liability originates
Structure prevention measures
Act strategically to reduce liabilities
Align legal, HR and management teams
nr1Psicossocial.differentialText: Integrates NR-1, mental health and law. Content applicable immediately, focused on liability prevention. Prevention is no longer optional.
nr1Psicossocial.ctaText: The lack of psychosocial risk management can lead to harassment and burnout convictions, significant indemnities and personal liability of managers.

tributosImobiliarios.title: Real Estate Taxation and Property Regularization
tributosImobiliarios.subtitle: A practical and strategic immersion on the main risks, taxes and opportunities in the real estate market.
tributosImobiliarios.intro: The real estate market demands much more than isolated legal knowledge. Today, property owners, investors, lawyers, accountants, consultants and managers need to understand how taxation, documentary regularization and asset structuring directly impact the security and profitability of operations. This course brings together, in clear language and with an objective focus, the central points that truly make a difference in practice.
targetList: Business owners, investors and asset managers
Asset holding and family holding managers

modules.title: Strategic Overview of Real Estate Taxation (1h)
items: Real estate taxation structure in Brazil
Tax competencies (Federal, State and Municipal)
Main property taxes
Practical logic of tax incidence

modules.title: Taxes in Practice: IPTU, ITR, ITBI, ITCMD and Income Tax (2h)
items: IPTU and ITR: practical differences and risks
ITBI: incidence, tax base and controversies
ITCMD: donation and inheritance
Capital gains tax (individuals and companies)
Common errors in real estate transactions

modules.title: Real Estate Transactions and Structuring (2h)
items: Purchase and sale, leasing (individuals vs. companies)
Capital contribution to holding companies and exchange
Real estate development
Comparison: individual, company and asset holding

modules.title: Tax Reform in the Real Estate Sector (1h30)
items: IBS and CBS in the real estate sector
Incidence on leasing, sale and assignment
Reducers: constitutional, legal and social
Optional 3.65% regime (transition)
Practical impacts on pricing and profitability

modules.title: Property Regularization (1h)
items: Title, deed and registration
Unregistered construction and occupancy permit
Municipal regularization
Adverse possession and compulsory adjudication
Most common property errors

modules.title: Asset Planning and Practical Cases (30min)
items: Asset holding and succession with properties
Asset protection
Real case analysis
Practical simulation

methodologyList: Integration of tax law, real estate law, property registration and asset planning
Objective analysis of tax reform impacts
resultsList: Identify tax and documentary risks in real estate transactions
Confidently analyze asset structures involving properties
Understand the effects of tax reform on the real estate market
Act with greater confidence in consulting, planning and decision-making
Guide clients, investors and companies with strategic vision
formatList: Mode: in-person, live online or hybrid
tributosImobiliarios.differentialText: In the real estate market, structuring mistakes are costly. This course integrates tax law, real estate law, property registration and asset planning for those who want to go beyond theory.
tributosImobiliarios.ctaText: Join this immersion and be prepared to act with greater confidence, strategy and vision for opportunity.
